reasoning: While programmatic and accessibility signals are strong, the fidelity report identifies a major visual duplication of header text and minor degradation of form field layout. Given that this is a fillable government form, the condensation of distinct labels into a run-on block and the redundant headers compromise professional standards and usability.

concerns.analysis: The header text content ('North Carolina Local Government Committee' and 'Hurricane Preparedness Checklist During Event Tasks') is visually duplicated at the start of both page content blocks. It appears first as part of the header graphic container and then immediately repeats as a plain-text heading block directly below the graphic. This redundant repetition is not present in the source layout, where the text appears only once within the header area of each page.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text 'North Carolina Local Government Committee / Hurricane Preparedness Checklist / During Event Tasks' appears exactly once at the top of Page 1 and Page 2.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows this text sequence once within the graphical header box and then again as a series of bold headings immediately below the box at the start of each page section.
concerns.location: Start of Page 1 and Page 2 content sections in HTML

concerns.analysis: The visual quality of the form labels at the beginning of the checklist is degraded. In the source PDF, 'Year:', 'Organization:', and 'Form Contact:' are formatted as distinct vertical labels with associated input lines. In the HTML output, these labels are condensed into a single run-on block of text ('Year: Organization: Form Contact:'), losing the visual clarity and separation required for form fields.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 shows 'Year:', 'Organization:', and 'Form Contact:' as three separate vertical fields on separate lines.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML rendering displays 'Year: Organization: Form Contact:' condensed into a single horizontal block of text.
concerns.location: Immediately following the repeated header text at the start of Page 1

action_items: Remove redundant header text repetitions caused by dual container rendering.
Restore visual separation and vertical formatting for the form identification labels.
